mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly increasing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0 percent, labor market deterioration signals severe demand contraction and recession risk, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses 8.0 percent upward.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer spending and construction investment, driving corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive momentum in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive return frequency above 58 percent over 60-day horizons.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side strength, which immediately boosts equity valuations over the following week during expansionary phases.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases yield negative 5-day forward returns in greater than 55 percent of observations during expansion regimes.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation indexed by CPIAUCSL breaches 230.0, anticipated aggressive monetary tightening compresses equity multiples and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in greater than 65 percent of periods following CPIAUCSL crossing 230.0.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate household income and consumer purchasing power, systematically lifting corporate revenues and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth correlates negatively with 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC below negative 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth decelerates below 1.0 percent while retail sales simultaneously contract, the dual demand shock triggers earnings downgrades and forces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarterly growth falls below 1.0 percent
failure_condition: The combined GDPC1 and RSAFS weakness fails to predict negative returns, with positive return frequency exceeding 50 percent in triggered states.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production surpassing the 95.0 index level signals peak capacity utilization and strong supply-side momentum, generating immediate positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: INDPRO readings above 95.0 are followed by negative 5-day returns in greater than 60 percent of cases.
